responses.\n77\tpub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n78\t\n79\t/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n80\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n81\tstruct QuorumState {\n82\t    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n83\t    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n84\t    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n85\t    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n86\t    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n87\t    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n88\t    /// Total degraded groups count\n89\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n90\t}\n91\t\n92\timpl QuorumState {\n93\t    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n94\t    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n95\t        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n99\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n100\t        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n104\t    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n105\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n106\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n110\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n111\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n112\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n113\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n114\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n115\t        met\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n119\t    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n120\t        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n121\t    }\n122\t\n123\t    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n124\t    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n125\t        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n126\t        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n127\t            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n128\t                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n129\t                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n130\t                    degraded += 1;\n131\t                }\n132\t            }\n133\t        }\n134\t        degraded\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n139\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n140\twhere\n141\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n142\t{\n143\t    Router::new()\n144\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n145\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n146\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n147\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n151\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n152\tasync  });\n164\t\n165\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\n185\tasync fn delete_documents(\n186\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n187\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n188\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n189\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n190\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n191\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n192\t        let s = ext.0;\n193\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n194\t    });\n195\t\n196\t    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n197\t    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n198\t        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n199\t            index_uid: index.clone(),\n200\t            filter: filter.clone(),\n201\t            origin: None, // Client write\n202\t        };\n203\t        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n204\t    }\n205\t\n206\t    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n207\t    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n208\t        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n209\t            .iter()\n210\t            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n211\t            .collect();\n212\t        if !ids.is_empty() {\n213\t            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n214\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n215\t                ids,\n216\t                origin: None, // Client write\n217\t            };\n218\t            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n219\t        }\n220\t    }\n221\t\n222\t    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n223\t    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n224\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n225\t        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n226\t    ))\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\n230\tasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n231\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n232\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n233\t    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n234\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n235\t    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n236\t        let s = ext.0;\n237\t        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n238\t    });\n239\t    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n240\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n241\t        ids: vec![id],\n242\t        origin: fields(index = %index, session_id))]\n249\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n250\t    index: String,\n251\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n252\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n253\t    state: &AppState,\n254\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n255\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n256\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n257\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n258\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n259\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n260\t        ));\n261\t    }\n262\t\n263\t    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n264\t    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n265\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n266\t        // Check if the index is an alias\n267\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n268\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n269\t            // It was an alias\n270\t            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n271\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n272\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n273\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n274\t                    format!(\n275\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n276\t                        index\n277\t                    ),\n278\t                ));\n279\t            }\n280\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n281\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n282\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n283\t        } else {\n284\t            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n285\t            index.clone()\n286\t        }\n287\t    } else {\n288\t        index.clone()\n289\t    };\n290\t\n291\t    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n292\t    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n293\t        documents\n294\t            .first()\n295\t            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n296\t    });\n297\t\n298\t    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n299\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n300\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n301\t            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n302\t        )\n303\t    })?;\n304\t\n305\t    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n306\t    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n307\t    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n308\t    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n309\t    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n310\t\n311\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n312\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n313\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n314\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n315\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n316\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n317\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n318\t            ));\n319\t        }\n320\t\n321\t        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n322\t        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n323\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n324\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n325\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n326\t            ));\n327\t        }\n328\t\n329\t        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n330\t        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n331\t        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n332\t        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n333\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n334\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n335\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n336\t            ));\n337\t        }\n338\t\n339\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n340\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n341\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n342\t                format!(\n343\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n344\t                    i, primary_key\n345\t                ),\n346\t            ));\n347\t        }\n348\t    }\n349\t\n350\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n351\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n352\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n353\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n354\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n355\t\n356\t    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n357\t    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n358\t        Some(\n359\t            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n360\t                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n361\t                .unwrap_or_default()\n362\t                .as_millis() as u64\n363\t        )\n364\t    } else {\n365\t        None\n366\t    };\n367\t\n368\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n369\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n370\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n371\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n372\t        }\n373\t\n374\t        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n375\t        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n376\t        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n377\t            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n378\t        }\n379\t    }\n380\t\n381\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n382\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n383\t\n384\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n385\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n386\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n387\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n388\t    );\n389\t\n390\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n391\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n392\t\n393\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n394\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n395\t        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n396\t        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n397\t            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n398\t            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n399\t                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n400\t                    c.read().await\n401\t                })\n402\t            })\n403\t        });\n404\t\n405\t        // Use migration-aware routing\n406\t        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n407\t            shard_id,\n408\t            &topology,\n409\t            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n410\t        );\n411\t\n412\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n413\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n414\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n415\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n416\t            ));\n417\t        }\n418\t\n419\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n420\t\n421\t        for node_id in targets {\n422\t            let node = topology\n423\t                .node(&node_id)\n424\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n425\t\n426\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n427\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n428\t\n429\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n430\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n431\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n432\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n433\t                origin: None, // Client write\n434\t            };\n435\t\n436\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n437\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n438\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n439\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n440\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n441\t                    }\n442\t                }\n443\t                Ok(resp) => {\n444\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n445\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n446\t                }\n447\t                Err(_) => {\n448\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n449\t                }\n450\t            }\n451\t        }\n452\t    }\n453\t\n454\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n455\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n456\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n457\t\n458\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n459\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n460\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n461\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n462\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n463\t        ));\n464\t    }\n465\t\n466\t    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n467\t    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n468\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n469\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n470\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n471\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n472\t            acks >= quorum\n473\t        })\n474\t        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n475\t\n476\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n477\t    let miroir_task = state\n478\t        .task_registry\n479\t        .register_with_metadata(\n480\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n481\t            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n482\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n483\t        )\n484\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n485\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n486\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n487\t        ))?;\n488\t\n489\t    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n490\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n491\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n492\t            sid,\n493\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n494\t            first_quorum_group,\n495\t        ).await {\n496\t            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n497\t            tracing::error!(\n498\t                session_id = %sid,\n499\t                error = %e,\n500\t                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n501\t            );\n502\t        }\n503\t    }\n504\t\n505\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n506\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n507\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n508\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n509\t            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n510\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n511\t            error: None,\n512\t            error_type: None,\n513\t            code: None,\n514\t            link: None,\n515\t        },\n516\t        degraded_groups,\n517\t    )\n518\t}\n519\t\n520\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n521\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n522\t    index: String,\n523\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n524\t    state: &AppState,\n525\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n526\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n527\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n528\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n529\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n530\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n531\t        ));\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n535\t    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n536\t        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n537\t        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n538\t            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n539\t            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n540\t                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n541\t                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n542\t                    format!(\n543\t                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n544\t                        index\n545\t                    ),\n546\t                ));\n547\t            }\n548\t            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n549\t            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n550\t            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n551\t        } else {\n552\t            index.clone()\n553\t        }\n554\t    } else {\n555\t        index.clone()\n556\t    };\n557\t\n558\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n559\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n560\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n561\t\n562\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n563\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n564\t    for id in &req.ids {\n565\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n566\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n567\t    }\n568\t\n569\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n570\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n571\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n572\t    );\n573\t\n574\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n575\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n576\t\n577\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n578\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n579\t        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n580\t\n581\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n582\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n583\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n584\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n585\t            ));\n586\t        }\n587\t\n588\t        for node_id in targets {\n589\t            let node = topology\n590\t                .node(&node_id)\n591\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n592\t\n593\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n594\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n595\t\n596\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n597\t                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n598\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n599\t                origin: None, // Client write\n600\t            };\n601\t\n602\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n603\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n604\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n605\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n606\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n607\t                    }\n608\t                }\n609\t                Ok(resp) => {\n610\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n611\t                }\n612\t                Err(_) => {\n613\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n614\t                }\n615\t            }\n616\t        }\n617\t    }\n618\t\n619\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n620\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n621\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n622\t\n623\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n624\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n625\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n626\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n627\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n628\t        ));\n629\t    }\n630\t\n631\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n632\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n633\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n634\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n635\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n636\t            acks >= quorum\n637\t        })\n638\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n639\t\n640\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n641\t    let miroir_task = state\n642\t        .task_registry\n643\t        .register_with_metadata(\n644\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n645\t            Some(index.clone()),\n646\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n647\t        )\n648\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n649\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n650\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n651\t        ))?;\n652\t\n653\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n654\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n655\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n656\t            sid,\n657\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n658\t            first_quorum_group,\n659\t        ).await {\n660\t            tracing::error!(\n661\t                session_id = %sid,\n662\t                error = %e,\n663\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n664\t            );\n665\t        }\n666\t    }\n667\t\n668\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n669\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n670\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n671\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n672\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n673\t            error: None,\n674\t            error_type: None,\n675\t            code: None,\n676\t            link: None,\n677\t        },\n678\t        degraded_groups,\n679\t    )\n680\t}\n681\t\n682\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n683\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n684\t    index: String,\n685\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n686\t    state: &AppState,\n687\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n688\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n689\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n690\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n691\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n692\t\n693\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n694\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n695\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n696\t    );\n697\t\n698\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n699\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n700\t\n701\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n702\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n703\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n704\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n705\t\n706\t        match client\n707\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n708\t            .await\n709\t        {\n710\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n711\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n712\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n713\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n714\t                }\n715\t            }\n716\t            Ok(resp) => {\n717\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n718\t            }\n719\t            Err(_) => {\n720\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n721\t            }\n722\t        }\n723\t    }\n724\t\n725\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n726\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n727\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n728\t\n729\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n730\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n731\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n732\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n733\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n734\t        ));\n735\t    }\n736\t\n737\t    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n738\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n739\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n740\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n741\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n742\t            acks >= quorum\n743\t        })\n744\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n745\t\n746\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n747\t    let miroir_task = state\n748\t        .task_registry\n749\t        .register_with_metadata(\n750\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n751\t            Some(index.clone()),\n752\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n753\t        )\n754\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n755\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n756\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n757\t        ))?;\n758\t\n759\t    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n760\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n761\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n762\t            sid,\n763\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n764\t            first_quorum_group,\n765\t        ).await {\n766\t            tracing::error!(\n767\t                session_id = %sid,\n768\t                error = %e,\n769\t                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n770\t            );\n771\t        }\n772\t    }\n773\t\n774\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n775\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n776\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n777\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n778\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n779\t            error: None,\n780\t            error_type: None,\n781\t            code: None,\n782\t            link: None,\n783\t        },\n784\t        degraded_groups,\n785\t    )\n786\t}\n787\t\n788\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n789\t///\n790\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n791\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n792\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n793\t        .iter()\n794\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n795\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n796\t}\n797\t\n798\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n799\t///\n800\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n801\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n802\t///\n803\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n804\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n805\t    documents: &[Value],\n806\t    primary_key: &str,\n807\t    topology: &Topology,\n808\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n809\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n810\t\n811\t    for doc in documents {\n812\t        let pk_value = doc\n813\t            .get(primary_key)\n814\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n815\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n816\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n817\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n818\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n819\t                )\n820\t            })?;\n821\t\n822\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n823\t        shard_documents\n824\t            .entry(shard_id)\n825\t            .or_default()\n826\t            .push(doc.clone());\n827\t    }\n828\t\n829\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n830\t}\n831\t\n832\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n833\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n834\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n835\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n836\t        indexUid: None,\n837\t        status: None,\n838\t        error: resp.message,\n839\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n840\t        code: resp.code,\n841\t        link: None,\n842\t    }\n843\t}\n844\t\n845\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n846\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n847\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n848\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n849\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n850\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n851\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n852\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n853\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n854\t        )\n855\t    })?;\n856\t\n857\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n858\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n859\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n860\t\n861\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n862\t    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n863\t        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n864\t    }\n865\t\n866\t    Ok(builder\n867\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n868\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n869\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n870\t            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n871\t        ))?)\n872\t}\n873\t\n874\t/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\n875\tfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n876\t    (\n877\t        StatusCode::OK,\n878\t        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n879\t        Json(resp),\n880\t    )\n881\t        .into_response()\n882\t}\n883\t\n884\t#[cfg(test)]\n885\tmod tests {\n886\t    use super::*;\n887\t    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, }\n1187\t}\n1188\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b537b289-61c3-4844-8659-e98909bc5ad7","uuid":"52fc2238-9974-4326-ad54-c03bc46004d5","timestamp":"2026-05-23T16:54:50.724Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/documents.rs","content":"//! Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::middleware::SessionId;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n            origin: None, // Client write\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    session_id: Option<Extension<crate::middleware::SessionId>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let sid = session_id.and_then(|ext| {\n        let s = ext.0;\n        if s.0.is_empty() { None } else { Some(s.0.clone()) }\n    });\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n        origin: None, // Client write\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state, sid).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 1. Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    // Aliases are resolved before any processing; writes to multi-target aliases are rejected\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        // Check if the index is an alias\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias\n            // Check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only, ILM-managed)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); writes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            // Not an alias, use the index name as-is\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard and _miroir_updated_at into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Get current timestamp in milliseconds since epoch for _miroir_updated_at stamping\n    let now_ms = if anti_entropy_enabled {\n        Some(\n            std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .as_millis() as u64\n        )\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n\n        // Stamp _miroir_updated_at when anti_entropy is enabled (plan §13.8)\n        // This happens AFTER reserved field validation, so orchestrator-controlled injection is allowed\n        if let Some(timestamp) = now_ms {\n            doc[updated_at_field] = serde_json::json!(timestamp);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index_uid.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Resolve alias to concrete index UID (plan §13.7)\n    let index_uid = if state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        let resolved = state.alias_registry.resolve(&index).await;\n        if resolved != vec![index.clone()] {\n            // It was an alias - check if it's a multi-target alias (read-only)\n            if state.alias_registry.is_multi_target_alias(&index).await {\n                return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n                    format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' is a multi-target alias and is read-only (managed by ILM); deletes must go to the concrete index or the write alias\",\n                        index\n                    ),\n                ));\n            }\n            // Single-target alias: record resolution metric and use the target\n            state.metrics.inc_alias_resolution(&index);\n            resolved.into_iter().next().unwrap_or_else(|| index.clone())\n        } else {\n            index.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        index.clone()\n    };\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n                origin: None, // Client write\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for delete by filter\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9157f7a2e43541cd94bcfa2e","type":"tool_result","content":"<system-reminder>[Truncated: PARTIAL view — showing lines 1-1437 of 3756 total (55529 tokens, cap 25000). Call Read with offset=1438 limit=1437 for the next page, or Grep to find a specific section. Do NOT answer from this page alone if the answer may be further in the file.]</system-reminder>\n\n1\t# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n2\t\n3\t**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n4\t\n5\tMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## Table of Contents\n10\t\n11\t1. [Design Principles](#1-design-principles)\n12\t2. [Architecture](#2-architecture)\n13\t3. [Document, Index, and Search Lifecycle](#3-document-index-and-search-lifecycle)\n14\t4. [Implementation](#4-implementation)\n15\t5. [API Surface and Compatibility](#5-api-surface-and-compatibility)\n16\t6. [Deployment](#6-deployment)\n17\t7. [CI/CD](#7-cicd)\n18\t8. [Testing](#8-testing)\n19\t9. [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n20\t10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n21\t11. [Onboarding](#11-onboarding)\n22\t12. [Delivered Artifacts](#12-delivered-artifacts)\n23\t13. [Advanced Capabilities](#13-advanced-capabilities)\n24\t14. [Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling](#14-resource-envelope-and-horizontal-scaling)\n25\t15. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n26\t\n27\t---\n28\t\n29\t## 1. Design Principles\n30\t\n31\t1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n32\t2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n33\t3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n34\t4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n35\t5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n36\t6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n37\t7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n38\t8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n39\t\n40\t### Key constraints\n41\t\n42\t- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n43\t- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n44\t- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n45\t- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n46\t\n47\t---\n48\t\n49\t## 2. Architecture\n50\t\n51\t### Core model\n52\t\n53\tMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n54\t\n55\t- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n56\t- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n57\t- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n58\t- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n59\t- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n60\t- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n61\t- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n62\t- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n63\t- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n64\t\n65\t```\n66\tClient\n67\t  │\n68\t  ▼\n69\tMiroir Orchestrator\n70\t  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n71\t  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n72\t  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n73\t\n74\t  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1\n75\t  ┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐\n76\t  │ node-0  node-1   │        │ node-3  node-4   │     ...  (RG groups)\n77\t  │ node-2           │        │ node-5           │\n78\t  └──────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘\n79\t  (each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes)\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\t### Replication factor\n83\t\n84\t| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n85\t|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n86\t| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n87\t| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n88\t| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n89\t\n90\t### Shard routing — Rendezvous hashing (HRW) within groups\n91\t\n92\tMiroir uses **Rendezvous hashing (HRW)** to assign shards to nodes. The assignment is scoped **within each replica group** — the rendezvous hash picks the top-RF nodes from a group's node list, not the global node list. This guarantees that every group independently forms a complete covering set with no cross-group shard overlap.\n93\t\n94\t```\n95\tnodes_in_group(g)           → the subset of nodes belonging to replica group g\n96\tassign_shard(shard_id, g)   → top RF nodes from nodes_in_group(g)\n97\t                              by score(hash(shard_id || node_id))\n98\t\n99\twrite_targets(shard_id)     → [assign_shard(shard_id, g) for g in 0..RG]\n100\t                              (flat union across all groups — RG × RF nodes total)\n101\t\n102\tsearch_group(query_seq)     → query_seq % RG   (round-robin across groups)\n103\tsearch_nodes(query)         → covering_set within search_group(query)\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\tWhy group-scoped assignment matters: if rendezvous ran over the global node list, both replicas of a shard might land on nodes in the same group, leaving the other group without coverage of that shard. Scoping to the group prevents this.\n107\t\n108\tProperties:\n109\t- On node addition within a group: only `~1/(Ng+1)` of that group's documents migrate, where Ng = nodes per group\n110\t- On node removal within a group: only `~RF/Ng` of that group's documents migrate\n111\t- Both operations leave other groups and all unaffected shards completely untouched\n112\t- No virtual nodes needed — rendezvous is naturally uniform\n113\t- Deterministic — any component can compute assignments from the same node list; no coordination required\n114\t\n115\tThe hash function is **twox-hash (xxhash family)** — the same function Meilisearch Enterprise uses for document sharding.\n116\t\n117\t### Write path\n118\t\n119\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n120\t2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n121\t3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n122\t4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n123\t5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n124\t6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n125\t7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n126\t8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n127\t\n128\t**Write quorum — two rules.**\n129\t\n130\t1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n131\t2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n132\t\n133\tExamples:\n134\t\n135\t| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n136\t|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n137\t| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n138\t| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n139\t| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n140\t| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n141\t| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n142\t\n143\tDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n144\t\n145\t**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n146\t\n147\t**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n148\t\n149\t### Read path\n150\t\n151\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n152\t2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n153\t3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n154\t4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n155\t5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n156\t6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n157\t7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n158\t8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n159\t9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n160\t\n161\tThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n162\t\n163\t**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement.
