      assert!(scatter_gather_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap().degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: None,\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            filter: None,\n            facets: None,\n            ranking_score: true, // Client requested scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({}),\n Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit = 10\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(10)));\n\n        // offset must be 0\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body handles large offset/limit correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_pagination() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 100,\n            limit: 50,\n            filter: None,\n offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16); // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // All nodes fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, should still fail when all groups are down\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, failed shards\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that partial policy does NOT use fallback.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_partial_policy_no_fallback() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Group 1 nodes are healthy but partial policy shouldn't use them\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\n NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With partial policy, should NOT use fallback\n shard_id * 2 + 1),\n154\t            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n155\t            \"_rankingScore\": 0.90,\n156\t        });\n157\t\n158\t        let hits = node_hits.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default();\n159\t        hits.push(doc1);\n160\t        hits.push(doc2);\n161\t        *node_totals.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 2;\n162\t\n163\t        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n164\t            total_docs: 100,\n165\t            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n166\t            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n167\t        });\n168\t    }\n169\t\n170\t    // Now build one response per node with all its accumulated docs\n171\t    for (node_id, hits) in node_hits {\n172\t        let total = node_totals.remove(&node_id).unwrap();\n173\t        mock.responses.insert(node_id, json!({\n174\t            \"hits\": hits,\n175\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n176\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n177\t        }));\n178\t    }\n179\t\n180\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n181\t    let req = SearchRequest {\n182\t        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n183\t        query: Some(\"unique-keyword\".to_string()),\n184\t        offset: 0,\n185\t        limit: 100,\n186\t        filter: None,\n187\t        facets: None,\n188\t        ranking_score: false,\n189\t        body: json!({}),\n190\t        global_idf: None,\n191\t    };\n192\t\n193\t    let result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n194\t        plan,\n195\t        &mock,\n196\t        req,\n197\t        &topo,\n198\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n199\t        &strategy,\n200\t    )\n201\t    .await\n202\t    .unwrap();\n203\t\n204\t    // Every document should appear exactly once (no duplicates)\n205\t    let mut seen_ids: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n206\t    for hit in &result.hits {\n207\t        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n208\t        *seen_ids.entry(id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n209\t    }\n210\t\n211\t    for (id, count) in &seen_ids {\n212\t        assert_eq!(*count, 1, \"doc {} appeared {} times, expected 1\", id, count);\n213\t    }\n214\t\n215\t    // Should find all 8 docs (2 per shard × 4 shards)\n216\t    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 8, \"expected 8 unique hits across 4 shards\");\n217\t}\n218\t\n219\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n220\t// DoD 3: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n221\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n222\t\n223\t#[test]\n224\tfn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n225\t    // Test the facet merge logic directly using the same algorithm the merger uses.\n226\t    // Since merge_facets is private, we replicate the merge logic here to validate\n227\t    // the aggregation contract.\n228\t    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n229\t\n230\t    // Shard 0: red=10, green=5, blue=3\n231\t    let mut shard0: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n232\t    let mut colors0: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n233\t    colors0.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 10);\n234\t    colors0.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 5);\n235\t    colors0.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 3);\n236\t    shard0.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors0);\n237\t\n238\t    // Shard 1: red=7, green=12, blue=8\n239\t    let mut shard1: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n240\t    let mut colors1: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n241\t    colors1.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 7);\n242\t    colors1.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 12);\n243\t    colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n244\t    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n245\t\n246\t    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n247\t    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n248\t    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n249\t        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n250\t            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n251\t            for (value, count) in values {\n252\t                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n253\t            }\n254\t        }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n258\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n259\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n260\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n264\t// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n265\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n266\t\n267\t#[tokio::test]\n268\tasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n269\t    let shards = 3u32;\n270\t    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n271\t\n272\t    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n273\t    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n274\t\n275\t    // Build covering set for page 1\n276\t    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n277\t\n278\t    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n279\t        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n280\t        for i in 0..5u32 {\n281\t            hits.push(json!({\n282\t                \"id\": format!(\"s{}-d{}\", shard_id, i),\n283\t                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n284\t                \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n285\t            }));\n286\t        }\n287\t        let response = json!({\n288\t            \"hits\": hits,\n289\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n290\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n291\t        });\n292\t        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n293\t        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n294\t            total_docs: 50,\n295\t            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n296\t            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n297\t        });\n298\t    }\n299\t\n300\t    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n301\t\n302\t    // Page 1: offset=0, limit=5\n303\t    let req1 = SearchRequest {\n304\t        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n305\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n306\t        offset: 0,\n307\t        limit: 5,\n308\t        filter: None,\n309\t        facets: None,\n310\t        ranking_score: true,\n311\t        body: json!({}),\n312\t        global_idf: None,\n313\t    };\n314\t    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n315\t        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n316\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n317\t    ).await.unwrap();\n318\t\n319\t    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n320\t    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n321\t    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n322\t    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n323\t        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n324\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n325\t        offset: 5,\n326\t        limit: 5,\n327\t        filter: None,\n328\t        facets: None,\n329\t        ranking_score: true,\n330\t        body: json!({}),\n331\t        global_idf: None,\n332\t    };\n333\t    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n334\t        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n335\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n336\t    ).await.unwrap();\n337\t\n338\t    // Pages should not overlap\n339\t    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n340\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n341\t        .collect();\n342\t    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n343\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n344\t        .collect();\n345\t\n346\t    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n347\t    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n348\t\n349\t    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n350\t    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n351\t    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n352\t\n353\t    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n354\t    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n355\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n356\t        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n357\t    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n358\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n359\t        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n360\t    assert!(\n361\t        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n362\t        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n363\t        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n364\t    );\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n368\t// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n369\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n370\t\n371\t#[test]\n372\tfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n373\t    let shards = 4u32;\n374\t    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n375\t\n376\t    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n377\t    for i in 2..4u32 {\n378\t        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n379\t            .unwrap()\n380\t            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n381\t            .unwrap();\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n385\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n386\t    for group in topo.groups() {\n387\t        if group.id == 1 {\n388\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n389\t            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n394\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n395\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n396\t        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n397\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n398\t                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n399\t            })\n400\t            .collect();\n401\t        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n402\t    }\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t#[test]\n406\tfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n407\t    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n408\t    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n409\t    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n410\t    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n411\t    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n412\t\n413\t    let rf = 2usize;\n414\t    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n415\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n416\t\n417\t    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n418\t    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n419\t    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n420\t\n421\t    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n422\t    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n423\t    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n424\t\n425\t    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n426\t    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n427\t        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n428\t    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n429\t\n430\t    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n431\t    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n432\t    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n436\t// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n437\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n438\t\n439\t#[test]\n440\tfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n441\t    // Every MiroirCode must produce a JSON object with exactly the keys:\n442\t    // {message, code, type, link} where code starts with \"miroir_\".\n443\t    for code in ALL_CODES {\n444\t        let err = MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n445\t        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n446\t        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n447\t\n448\t        // Must have all four fields\n449\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n450\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing code\", code);\n451\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing type\", code);\n452\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing link\", code);\n453\t\n454\t        // Code must start with miroir_\n455\t        let code_str = parsed[\"code\"].as_str().unwrap();\n456\t        assert!(\n457\t            code_str.starts_with(\"miroir_\"),\n458\t            \"{:?}: code '{}' should start with miroir_\",\n459\t            code, code_str\n460\t        );\n461\t\n462\t        // Type must be a valid Meilisearch error type\n463\t        let type_str = parsed[\"type\"].as_str().unwrap();\n464\t        assert!(\n465\t            [\"invalid_request\", \"auth\", \"internal\", \"system\"].contains(&type_str),\n466\t            \"{:?}: type '{}' is not a valid Meilisearch error type\",\n467\t            code, type_str\n468\t        );\n469\t\n470\t        // Link must be a string pointing to docs\n471\t        let link_str = parsed[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap();\n472\t        assert!(\n473\t            link_str.contains(\"docs/errors.md\"),\n474\t            \"{:?}: link let meili_body = r#\"{\n483\t        \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n484\t        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n485\t        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n486\t        \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#index_not_found\"\n487\t    }\"#;\n488\t\n489\t    let err = MeilisearchError::forwarded(meili_body).unwrap();\n490\t    let roundtrip = assert_eq!(original[\"type\"], result[\"type\"]);\n497\t    assert_eq!(original[\"link\"], result[\"link\"]);\n498\t}\n499\t\n500\t#[test]\n501\tfn test_forwarded_document_not_found_error() {\n502\t    let meili_body = r#\"{\n503\t        \"message\": \"Document `abc123` not found.\",\n504\t        \"code\": \"document_not_found\",\n505\t        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n506\t use miroir_proxy::routes::admin_endpoints::{NodeInfo, TopologyResponse};\n535\t\n536\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n537\t        shards: 64,\n538\t        replication_factor: 2,\n539\t        nodes: vec![\n540\t            NodeInfo {\n541\t                id: \"node-0\".to_string(),\n542\t                address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\".to_string(),\n543\t                status: assert!(nodes[0].get(\"error\").is_none());\n595\t}\n596\t\n597\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n598\t// Additional: search response field miroir_proxy::routes::search::strip_internal_fields;\n604\t\n605\t    // Case 1: _miroir_shard and _rankingScore both present, client didn't request score\n606\t    let mut hit = json!({\n607\t        \"id\": \"doc-1\",\n608\t        \"title\": \"Test Document\",\n609\t        \"_miroir_shard\": 3,\n610\t        \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n611\t    });\n612\t    strip_internal_fields(&mut hit, false);\n613\t    assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"_miroir_shard should be stripped\");\n614\t    assert!(hit.get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none(), \"_rankingScore should be stripped when not requested\");\n615\t\n616\t    // Case 2: client requested ranking score\n617\t    let mut hit2 = json!({\n618\t        \"id\": \"doc-2\",\n619\t        \"title\": \"Another Document\",\n620\t        \"_miroir_shard\": 5,\n621\t        \"_rankingScore\": 0.88,\n622\t    });\n623\t    strip_internal_fields(&mut hit2, true);\n624\t    assert!(hit2.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"_miroir_shard ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n629\t// Additional: reserved field contract\n630\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n631\t\n632\t#[test]\n633\tfn test_reserved_field_rejection() {\n634\t    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n635\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n636\t        \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n637\t    );\n638\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n639\t\n640\t    assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n641\t    assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n642\t    assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n643\t}\n644\t\n645\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n646\t// Additional: auth error shape matches Meilisearch\n647\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n648\t\n649\t#[test]\n650\tfn test_auth_error_shapes_match_meilisearch() {\n651\t    let err = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::InvalidAuth, \"The provided authorization is invalid.\");\n652\t    assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 401);\n653\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n654\t    assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"auth\");\n655\t\n656\t    let err = MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::JwtInvalid, \"JWT signature verification ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn two_group_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 2, 2);\n    for i in 0..2u32 MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 1: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_shard_assignment_coverage() {\n    let shards = 8u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let pk = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&pk, shards);\n        shard_counts.entry(shard).or_default().push(pk);\n    }\n\n    // Every shard should have at least one document\n    assert_eq!(shard_counts.len(), shards as usize, \"all shards should receive documents\");\n\n    // All write targets should be reachable\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 2: unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_deduplication() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Compute covering set for query_seq=0\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, shards);\n\n    // Build per-node responses by accumulating all docs for each node.\n    // Multiple shards may map to the same node; a real Meilisearch node\n    // returns all its matching docs in a single response.\n    let mut node_hits: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut node_totals: HashMap<NodeId, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        let doc1 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-a\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n        });\n        let doc2 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-b\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2 + 1),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.90,\n        });\n\n        let hits = node_hits.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default();\n        hits.push(doc1);\n        hits.push(doc2);\n        *node_totals.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 2;\n\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Now build one response per node with all its accumulated docs\n    for (node_id, hits) in node_hits {\n        let total = node_totals.remove(&node_id).unwrap();\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id, json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }));\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"unique-keyword\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 100,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &mock,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Every document should appear exactly once (no duplicates)\n    let mut seen_ids: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    for hit in &result.hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n        *seen_ids.entry(id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    for (id, count) in &seen_ids {\n        assert_eq!(*count, 1, \"doc {} appeared {} times, expected 1\", id, count);\n    }\n\n    // Should find all 8 docs (2 per shard × 4 shards)\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 8, \"expected 8 unique hits across 4 shards\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 3: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    // Test the facet merge logic directly using the same algorithm the merger uses.\n    // Since merge_facets is private, we replicate the merge logic here to validate\n    // the aggregation contract.\n    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n    // Shard 0: red=10, green=5, blue=3\n    let mut shard0: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors0: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors0.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 10);\n    colors0.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 5);\n    colors0.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 3);\n    shard0.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors0);\n\n    // Shard 1: red=7, green=12, blue=8\n    let mut shard1: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors1: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors1.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 7);\n    colors1.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 12);\n    colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n\n    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n            for (value, count) in values {\n                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let shards = 3u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Build covering set for page 1\n    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..5u32 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"s{}-d{}\", shard_id, i),\n                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n                \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n            }));\n        }\n        let response = json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n        });\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Page 1: offset=0, limit=5\n    let req1 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 5,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Pages should not overlap\n    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n\n    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n\n    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n    assert!(\n        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        if group.id == 1 {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n\n    let rf = 2usize;\n    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n\n    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n\n    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n\n    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n\n    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n    // Every MiroirCode must produce a JSON object with exactly the keys:\n    // {message, code, type, link} where code starts with \"miroir_\".\n    for code in ALL_CODES {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n\n        // Must have all four fields\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing code\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing type\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing link\", code);\n\n        // Code must start with miroir_\n        let code_str = parsed[\"code\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            code_str.starts_with(\"miroir_\"),\n            \"{:?}: code '{}' should start with miroir_\",\n            code, code_str\n        );\n\n        // Type must be a valid Meilisearch error type\n        let type_str = parsed[\"type\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            [\"invalid_request\", \"auth\", \"internal\", \"system\"].contains(&type_str),\n            \"{:?}: type '{}' is not a valid Meilisearch error type\",\n            code, type_str\n        );\n\n        // Link must be a string pointing to docs\n        let link_str = parsed[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            link_str.contains(\"docs/errors.md\"),\n            \"{:?}: link '{}' should point to error docs\",\n            code, link_str\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_meilisearch_error_preserves_shape() {\n    let meili_body = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n        \"type\": assert!(parsed[\"rebalance_in_progress\"].is_boolean());\n    assert!(parsed[\"fully_covered\"].is_boolean());\n\n    // Validate node shape\n    let nodes = parsed[\"nodes\"].as_array().unwrap();\n assert!(nodes[0].get(\"error\").is_none());\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Additional: search response field stripping\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_search_response_strips_internal_fields() {\n    use miroir_proxy::routes::search::strip_internal_fields;\n\n    // Case 1: _miroir_shard and _rankingScore both present, client didn't request score\n    let mut hit = json!({\n        \"id\": \"doc-1\",\n        \"title\": \"Test Document\",\n        \"_miroir_shard\": 3,\n        \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n    });\n    strip_internal_fields(&mut hit, false);\n    assert!(hit.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"_miroir_shard should be stripped\");\n    assert!(hit.get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none(), \"_rankingScore should be stripped when not requested\");\n\n    // Case 2: client requested ranking score\n    let mut hit2 = json!({\n        \"id\": \"doc-2\",\n        \"title\": \"Another Document\",\n        \"_miroir_shard\": 5,\n        \"_rankingScore\": 0.88,\n    });\n    strip_internal_fields(&mut hit2, true);\n    assert!(hit2.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none(), \"_miroir_shard should always be stripped\");\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1deedeb94c694b879cc9f5e4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\n4\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n6\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n7\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n8\t\n9\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n12\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n13\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n14\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n15\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    h.finish()\n17\t}\n18\t\n19\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n20\t///\n21\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n24\t/// for deterministic resolution.\n25\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n26\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n27\t        .iter()\n28\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n29\t        .collect();\n30\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n31\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n32\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n33\t    });\n34\t    scored\n35\t        .into_iter()\n36\t        .take(rf)\n37\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n42\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n43\t    topology\n44\t        .groups()\n45\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n46\t        .collect()\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n50\t///\n51\t/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;\n101\t    }\n102\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n106\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n107\t    (0..shard_count)\n108\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n109\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n110\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n111\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n112\t        })\n113\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n114\t        .into_iter()\n115\t        .collect()\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n119\t///\n120\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n121\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n122\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n123\t    shard_count: u32,\n124\t    group: &Group,\n125\t    rf: usize,\n126\t    query_seq: u64,\n127\t    index: &str,\n128\t    floor: u64,\n129\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n130\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n131\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n132\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n133\t\n134\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n136\t\n137\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n138\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n139\t            .iter()\n140\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n141\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n142\t                version >= floor\n143\t            })\n144\t            .collect();\n145\t\n146\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n147\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n148\t            return None;\n149\t        }\n150\t\n151\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n152\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n153\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n154\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    Some(result)\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n162\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n163\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n164\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n165\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n169\t///\n170\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n171\t/// For each shard, change once)\n185\t            for node in new_nodes {\n186\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n187\t                    diff += 1;\n188\t                }\n189\t            }\n190\t }\n506\t\n507\t    /// P1.3-A3: covering_set covers all shards within the chosen group.\n508\t    #[test]\n509\t    fn test_covering_set_covers_all_shards() {\n510\t        let mut topo =    }\n534\t    }\n535\t\n536\t    /// P1.3-A4: covering_set size is bounded by Ng (nodes in group).\n537\t    #[test]\n538\t    fn test_covering_set_size_bound() {\n539\t        let mut topo = outputs.\n561\t    #[test]\n562\t    fn test_covering_set_determinism() {\n563\t        let mut topo1 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n564\t        let mut topo2 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n565\t\n566\t        for i in 0u32..6 {\n567\t            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n568\t            let node = Node::new(\n569\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n570\t       let g1 = topo1.group(0).unwrap();\n578\t        let g2 = topo2.group(0).unwrap();\n579\t\n580\t        for query_seq in 0..10 {\n581\t            let c1 = covering_set(64, g1, 2, std::collections::HashSet<_> = c1.into_iter().collect();\n585\t            let s2: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c2.into_iter().collect();\n586\t            assert_eq!(\n587\t                s1, s2,\n588\t                \"covering_set differs for identical topologies at query_seq={}\",\n589\t                query_seq\n590\t            );\n591\t        }\n592\t    }\n593\t\n594\t    /// P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n625\t            chi_square,\n626\t            critical_value\n627\t        );\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set rotates replicas by query_seq.\n631\t    #[test]\n632\t    fn test_covering_set_rotates_replicas() {\n633\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(8, 1, 3);\n634\t        for i in 0u32..4 {\n635\t            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n636\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n637\t                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n638\t                0,\n639\t            ));\n640\t        }\n641\t\n642\t        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n643\t        let c0 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 0);\n644\t        let c1 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 1);\n645\t        let c2 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n646\t\n647\t        // For each shard, verify that the selected node rotates\n648\t        for shard_id in 0..8 {\n649\t            let replicas =
