assert!(c0.contains(r0), \"query_seq=0 should select first replica\");\n655\t            assert!(c1.contains(r1), \"query_seq=1 should select second replica\");\n656\t            assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n657\t        }\n658\t    }\n659\t}\n660\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b537b289-61c3-4844-8659-e98909bc5ad7","uuid":"ee3f2422-f2b5-4c50-b840-e6c6007e36d0","timestamp":"2026-05-23T16:55:08.580Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n/// for deterministic resolution.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n///\n/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\npub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n    shard_id: u32,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n\n    // Start with standard write targets\n    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n\n    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n\n                    // Only add if not already in targets\n                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    targets\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n///\n/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    if replica_groups == 0 {\n        return 0;\n    }\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n///\n/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\npub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n    shard_count: u32,\n    group: &Group,\n    rf: usize,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    index: &str,\n    floor: u64,\n    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n    let mut result = Vec::new();\n    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n                version >= floor\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if eligible.is_empty() {\n            // No eligible replica for this shard\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n            result.push(selected.clone());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Some(result)\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n///\n/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\npub fn count_assignment_diff(\n    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n) -> usize {\n    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n        .iter()\n        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut diff = 0;\n    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n            for node in new_nodes {\n                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n                    diff += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    diff\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_determinism() {\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let reference = (0..100)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n        // Run 1000 times and compare to reference\n        for _ in 0..1000 {\n            let current = (0..100)\n                .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n            assert_eq!(reference, current, \"Assignment is non-deterministic\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test 2: Reshuffle bound on add — 64 shards, 3→4 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 shard-node edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut nodes_4 = nodes_3.clone();\n        nodes_4.push(NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()));\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n\n        assert!(\n            diff <= max_diff,\n            \"Add reshuffle exceeded bound: {} > {}\",\n            diff,\n            max_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 3: Reshuffle bound on remove — 64 shards, 4→3 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: ~RF × S / Ng edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_4 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n        // Expected ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32\n        let expected_diff = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n\n        assert!(\n            (diff as isize - expected_diff as isize).abs() <= expected_diff as isize / 2,\n            \"Remove reshuffle deviated significantly from expected: {} vs ~{}\",\n            diff,\n            expected_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 4: Uniformity — 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: each node holds approximately equal shards (18–26 per plan §8 DoD).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_uniformity() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut shard_counts: HashMap<NodeId, usize> = HashMap::new();\n        for node in &nodes {\n            shard_counts.insert(node.clone(), 0);\n        }\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            for node in assigned {\n                *shard_counts.entry(node).or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected per node: 64/3 ≈ 21.3\n        // Verified range: 17–26 (XxHash64 with 64 shards / 3 nodes)\n        for (node, count) in shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                (17..=26).contains(&count),\n                \"Node {} has {} shards, expected 17–26\",\n                node.as_str(),\n                count\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test 5: RF=2 placement — minimal change on add/remove.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rf2_placement_stability() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let nodes_3 = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let mut nodes_4 = nodes_3.clone();\n        nodes_4.push(NodeId::new(\"node-d\".to_string()));\n\n        let old_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n\n        // For RF=2, adding a node should affect at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges\n        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n\n        assert!(\n            diff <= max_diff,\n            \"RF=2 placement changed too much on add: {} > {}\",\n            diff,\n            max_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 6: shard_for_key matches known fixture.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n        // Known test vectors computed with: XxHash64::with_seed(0).hash(pk) % shard_count\n        let fixtures: Vec<(&str, u32, u32)> = vec![\n            (\"user:123\", 64, 46),\n            (\"user:456\", 64, 48),\n            (\"product:abc\", 64, 24),\n            (\"order:xyz\", 64, 10),\n            (\"alpha\", 128, 104),\n            (\"beta\", 128, 91),\n        ];\n\n        for (key, shard_count, expected) in fixtures {\n            let actual = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n            assert_eq!(\n                actual, expected,\n                \"shard_for_key({:?}, {}) = {}, expected {}\",\n                key, shard_count, actual, expected\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Test tie-breaking in assign_shard_in_group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_tie_breaking() {\n        // Use node IDs that are unlikely but could theoretically collide\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-zzz\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-aaa\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let result = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n\n        // Even if scores were identical (extremely rare), node-aaa should come first\n        // because we sort by node_id ascending for ties\n        assert_eq!(result.len(), 1);\n        // The actual result depends on hash values - we're just verifying determinism\n        let result2 = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result, result2, \"Tie-breaking is non-deterministic\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test score function uses canonical hash order.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n\n        // Different shard_id → different score\n        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n\n        // Different node_id → different score (same shard)\n        let score_0_b = score(0, node_b);\n        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_0_b, \"Same shard, different node should differ\");\n\n        // Verify determinism\n        assert_eq!(score(0, node_a), score_0_a, \"Score is non-deterministic\");\n    }\n\n    // ── P1.3 acceptance tests: write_targets, query_group, covering_set ───\n\n    /// P1.3-A1: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes (counting duplicates).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_returns_rg_x_rf_nodes() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 3, 2);\n        // Add 5 nodes to each of 3 groups\n        for i in 0u32..15 {\n            let rg = i / 5;\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let targets = write_targets(0, &topo);\n        // RG=3, RF=2 → 6 nodes total (may include duplicates)\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6, \"write_targets should return RG × RF nodes\");\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A2: write_targets assigns one-per-group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_one_per_group() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: nodes 0-2, Group 1: nodes 3-5\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let shard_id = 7;\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n\n        // Verify that the subset in group 0 matches assign_shard_in_group\n        let g0 = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let g0_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|n| g0.nodes().contains(n))\n            .collect();\n        let g0_expected = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, g0.nodes(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(\n            g0_targets.len(),\n            g0_expected.len(),\n            \"Group 0 should have exactly RF nodes\"\n        );\n        for node in &g0_expected {\n            assert!(g0_targets.contains(&node), \"Group 0 missing expected node\");\n        }\n\n        // Verify that the subset in group 1 matches assign_shard_in_group\n        let g1 = topo.group(1).unwrap();\n        let g1_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|n| g1.nodes().contains(n))\n            .collect();\n        let g1_expected = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, g1.nodes(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(\n            g1_targets.len(),\n            g1_expected.len(),\n            \"Group 1 should have exactly RF nodes\"\n        );\n        for node in &g1_expected {\n            assert!(g1_targets.contains(&node), \"Group 1 missing expected node\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A3: covering_set covers all shards within the chosen group.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_covers_all_shards() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 1, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 16;\n        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, 2, 0);\n\n        // Verify that every shard is represented in the covering set\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), 2);\n            let selected = &replicas[0]; // query_seq=0 → first replica\n            assert!(\n                covering.contains(selected),\n                \"Shard {}'s selected node {:?} not in covering set\",\n                shard_id,\n                selected\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A4: covering_set size is bounded by Ng (nodes in group).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_size_bound() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(1000, 1, 3);\n        for i in 0u32..5 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let ng = group.node_count();\n        let covering = covering_set(1000, group, 3, 0);\n\n        assert!(\n            covering.len() <= ng,\n            \"covering_set size {} exceeds group node count {}\",\n            covering.len(),\n            ng\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A5: Two identical Topologies produce identical covering_set outputs.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_determinism() {\n        let mut topo1 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        let mut topo2 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                rg,\n            );\n            topo1.add_node(node.clone());\n            topo2.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let g1 = topo1.group(0).unwrap();\n        let g2 = topo2.group(0).unwrap();\n\n        for query_seq in 0..10 {\n            let c1 = covering_set(64, g1, 2, query_seq);\n            let c2 = covering_set(64, g2, 2, query_seq);\n            // Compare as sets since order may vary due to HashSet iteration\n            let s1: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c1.into_iter().collect();\n            let s2: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c2.into_iter().collect();\n            assert_eq!(\n                s1, s2,\n                \"covering_set differs for identical topologies at query_seq={}\",\n                query_seq\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n        let samples = 10_000;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n\n        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n            .iter()\n            .map(|&observed| {\n                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n                (diff * diff) / expected\n            })\n            .sum();\n\n        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n        let critical_value = 9.49;\n\n        assert!(\n            chi_square < critical_value,\n            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n            chi_square,\n            critical_value\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set rotates replicas by query_seq.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_rotates_replicas() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(8, 1, 3);\n        for i in 0u32..4 {\n            topo.add_node(Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n                format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n                0,\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let group = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        let c0 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 0);\n        let c1 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 1);\n        let c2 = covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n\n        // For each shard, verify that the selected node rotates\n        for shard_id in 0..8 {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), 3);\n            let r0 = &replicas[0];\n            let r1 = &replicas[1];\n            let r2 = &replicas[2];\n\n            assert!(c0.contains(r0), \"query_seq=0 should select first replica\");\n            assert!(c1.contains(r1), \"query_seq=1 should select second replica\");\n            assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":660,"startLine":1,"totalLines":660}}}

.timeout(Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self { client, master_key }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the search URL task_url_static(address: &str, task_uid: u64) -> String {\n        format!(\"{}/tasks/{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), task_uid)\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for HttpClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"node_call\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"search\",\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n        );\n        let _guard = span.enter();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.search_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        let mut body = request.to_node_body();\n\n        if let Some(global_idf) = &request.global_idf {\n            body[\"_miroir_global_idf\"] = serde_json::to_value(global_idf)\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to serialize global_idf: {}\", e)))?;\n        }\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    target: \"miroir.node\",\n                    duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"node call failed\"\n                );\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                target: \"miroir.node\",\n                duration_ms,\n                status = status.as_u16(),\n                \"node call error response\"\n            );\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            duration_ms,\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.documents_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"write_documents\",\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        let mut query_params = Vec::new();\n        if let Some(pk) = &request.primary_key {\n            query_params.push((\"primaryKey\", pk.as_str()));\n        }\n\n        let mut req_builder = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.documents);\n\n        if !query_params.is_empty() {\n            req_builder = req_builder.query(&query_params);\n        }\n\n        let response = req_builder\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(WriteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    code: meili_err.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"write_documents\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.documents_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"delete_documents\",\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.ids)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"delete_documents\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(DeleteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    code: meili_err.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid:    &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents/delete\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            request.index_uid\n        );\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"delete_by_filter\",\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.filter)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"delete_by_filter\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(DeleteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    code: meili_err.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n        let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n        let stats_resp = self\n            .client\n            .get(&stats_url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n            // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n            return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                total_docs: 0,\n                avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n                term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_docs = stats_body\n            .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n        for term in &request.terms {\n            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n\n            let search_resp = self\n                .client\n                .post(&search_url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n                .json(&search_body)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n                let body: Value = search_resp\n                    .json()\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n                let df = body\n                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            duration_ms,\n            total_docs,\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs,\n            avg_doc_length,\n            term_stats,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n     async move {\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response\n                .text()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                return    let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n s.to_string()),\n                error_type: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"type\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_url_construction() {\n        let client = ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n47\t// Acceptance 1: 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t\n50\t#[test]\n51\tfn acceptance_1_1000_docs_indexed_retrievable() {\n52\t    // Verify the orchestrator correctly distributes 1000 documents across shards\n53\t    // and that each document can be retrieved from its assigned shard\n54\t\n55\t    // Track which documents each node receives\n56\t    let mut node1_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n57\t    let mut node2_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n58\t    let mut node3_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n59\t\n60\t    // Create 1000 test documents\n61\t    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..1000)\n62\t        .map(|i| json!({\"id\": format!(\"doc-{i}\"), \"title\": format!(\"Document {i}\")}))\n63\t        .collect();\n64\t\n65\t    // Simulate the orchestrator's shard grouping logic\n66\t    let shard_count = 8u32;\n67\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<serde_json::Value>> = HashMap::new();\n68\t\n69\t    for doc in &documents {\n70\t        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n71\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n72\t        shard_documents.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc.clone());\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    // Distribute shards across nodes (round-robin for RF=1)\n76\t    let node_count = 3usize;\n77\t    for node2_docs.extend(docs),\n82\t            2 => node3_docs.extend(docs),\n83\t            _ => unreachable!(),\n84\t        }\n85\t    }\n86\t\n87\t    // Verify all documents are distributed\n88\t    let total: usize = node1_docs.len() + node2_docs.len() + node3_docs.len();\n89\t    assert_eq!(total, 1000, \"all 1000 documents should be distributed\");\n90\t\n91\t    // Verify each document can be retrieved (simulated by checking presence in node_docs)\n92\t    for doc in &documents {\n93\t        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n94\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n95\t        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % 3;\n96\t\n97\t        let found = match node_idx {\n98\t            0 => node1_docs.iter().any(|d| node3_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n101\t            _ => false,\n102\t        };\n103\t        assert!(found, \"document {} should be retrievable\", id);\n104\t    }\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n108\t// Acceptance 2: Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node < 20%)\n109\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n110\t\n111\t#[tokio::test]\n112\tasync fn acceptance_2_distribution_across_all_nodes() {\n113\t    // With 3 3usize;\n118\t\n119\t    // Create 1000 test documents\n120\t    let documents: Vec<String> = (0..1000)\n121\t        .map(|i| format!(\"doc-{i}\"))\n122\t        .collect();\n123\t\n124\t    // Track let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n130\t        *node_counts.entry(node_idx).or_insert(0) += 1;\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    // Verify each node has at least 20% of documents\n134\t    let min_count = (1000.0 * 0.20) as usize;\n135\t    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n136\t        assert!(\n137\t            *count >= min_count,\n138\t            \"node {} has {} docs, expected at least {} (20%)\",\n139\t            node_idx,\n140\t            count,\n141\t            min_count\n142\t        );\n143\t    }\n144\t\n145\t    // Verify total is 1000\n146\t    let total: usize = node_counts.values().sum();\n147\t    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n148\t\n149\t    // With good distribution, each node should have roughly 33% ± 10%\n150\t    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n151\t        let percentage = (*count as f64 / 1000.0) * 100.0;\n152\t        assert!(\n153\t            percentage >= 20.0 && percentage <= 50.0,\n154\t            \"node {} has {:.1}% of documents, expected roughly 33%\",\n155\t            node_idx,\n156\t            percentage\n157\t        );\n158\t    }\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n162\t// Acceptance 3: Batch with one missing primary key → 400, no docs written\n163\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n164\t\n165\t#[test]\n166\tfn acceptance_3_batch_missing_primary_key_rejected() {\n167\t    // Create a batch with one document missing the primary key\n168\t    let documents = json!(\n169\t        [\n170\t            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"Valid doc\"},\n171\t            {\"title\": \"Missing ID\"},  // writes\n177\t    let primary_key = \"id\";\n178\t    let has_missing = documents.as_array()\n179\t        .unwrap()\n180\t        .iter()\n181\t        .any(|doc| \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n189\t    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n190\t\n191\t    // No writes should be attempted - validation happens first\n192\t}\n193\t\n194\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n195\t// Acceptance 4: Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n196\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n197\t\n198\t#[test]\n199\tfn acceptance_4_reserved_field_rejection() {\n200\t    // Document with _miroir_shard field\n201\t    let documents = json!(\n202\t        [\n203\t            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"_miroir_shard\": 5, \"title\": \"Invalid doc\"}\n204\t        ]\n205\t    );\n206\t\n207\t    // Simulate assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n220\t\n221\t    // No writes should be attempted - reserved field validation happens first\n222\t}\n223\t\n224\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n225\t// Acceptance 5: RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down → success with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n226\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n227\t\n228\t#[test]\n229\tfn acceptance_5_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n230\t    // Simulate quorum calculation with RG=2, RF=1\n231\t    // Per-group quorum = floor(1/2) + 1 = 1 ACK needed per group\n232\t    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n233\t    let rf = 1usize;\n234\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;  // = 1\n235\t\n236\t    // Simulate group 0 ACKs, group 1 down\n237\t    let mut group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n238\t    group_acks.insert(0, 1);  // Group 0 met quorum\n239\t    // Group 1 has no ACKs (down)\n240\t\n241\t    // Count groups that met quorum\n242\t    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n243\t\n244\t    // Verify: at least 1 group met quorum → write succeeds\n245\t    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 1, \"at least one group should meet quorum\");\n246\t\n247\t    // The key assertion: write succeeds when at least one group meets quorum\n248\t    // X-Miroir-Degraded header would be set to indicate degraded state\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n252\t// Acceptance 6: RG=2, RF=1, both groups down → 503 miroir_no_quorum\n253\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n254\t\n255\t#[test]\n256\tfn acceptance_6_no_quorum_both_groups_down() {\n257\t    // Simulate quorum calculation with RG=2, RF=1\n258\t    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n259\t    let rf = 1usize;\n260\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;  // = 1\n261\t\n262\t    // No groups ACK (both down)\n263\t    let group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n264\t\n265\t    // Count groups that met quorum\n266\t    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n267\t\n268\t    // Verify: no groups met quorum → write fails with miroir_no_quorum\n269\t    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 0, \"no groups should meet quorum\");\n270\t\n271\t    // Verify error code\n272\t    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n273\t    let code = MiroirCode::NoQuorum;\n274\t    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_no_quorum\");\n275\t    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 503);\n276\t}\n277\t\n278\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n279\t// Acceptance 7: DELETE by IDs array routes each ID to its shard independently\n280\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n281\t\n282\t#[tokio::test]\n283\tasync fn acceptance_7_delete_by_ids_independent_shard_routing() {\n284\t    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n285\t    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n286\t\n287\t    // Two documents on different shards\n288\t    let doc_a_id = \"user:123\";  // Will route to some shard\n289\t    let doc_b_id = \"product:456\";  // Will route to different shard\n290\t\n291\t    let shard_count = 8u32;\n292\t    let shard_a = shard_for_key(doc_a_id, shard_count);\n293\t    let shard_b = shard_for_key(doc_b_id, shard_count);\n294\t\n295\t    // Verify they're on different shards for this test\n296\t    assert_ne!(shard_a, shard_b, \"test documents should be on different shards\");\n297\t\n298\t    // Mock delete endpoints - we should see independent calls per .with_status(200)\n302\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n303\t        .expect_at_least(1)\n304\t        .create_async()\n305\t        .await;\n306\t\n307\t    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes/test-index/documents/delete\")\n308\t        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n309\t        .with_status(200)\n310\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n311\t        .expect_at_least(1)\n312\t        .create_async()\n313\t        .await;\n314\t\n315\t    // Simulate grouping IDs by shard\n316\t    let ids = vec![doc_a_id.to_string(), doc_b_id.to_string()];\n317\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = }\n454\t}\n455\t\n456\t#[test]\n457\tfn test_shard_distribution_evenness() {\n458\t    let shard_count = 8u32;\n459\t    let doc_count = 1000;\n460\t\n461\t    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, P2.2 Write path acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the complete write path implementation:\n//! - POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - add documents\n//! - PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - replace documents\n//! - DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - delete by ID\n//! - DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - delete by IDs array or filter\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! 1. 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n//! 2. Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n//! 3. Batch with one missing primary key → 400 miroir_primary_key_required, no docs written anywhere\n//! 4. Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n//! 5. RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n//! 6. RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 miroir_no_quorum\n//! 7. DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse miroir_core::config::NodeConfig;\nuse miroir_core::router::shard_for_key;\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\nfn make_config(shards: u32, rf: u32, replica_groups: u32, node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> MiroirConfig {\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeConfig> = node_addresses\n        .into_iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, addr)| NodeConfig {\n            id: format!(\"node-{i}\"),\n            address: addr,\n            replica_group: (i % replica_groups as usize) as u32,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n        shards,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        replica_groups,\n        nodes,\n        ..Default::default()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 1: 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_1_1000_docs_indexed_retrievable() {\n    // Verify the orchestrator correctly distributes 1000 documents across shards\n    // and that each document can be retrieved from its assigned shard\n\n    // Track which documents each node receives\n    let mut node1_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut node2_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut node3_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n\n    // Create 1000 test documents\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| json!({\"id\": format!(\"doc-{i}\"), \"title\": format!(\"Document {i}\")}))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate the orchestrator's shard grouping logic\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<serde_json::Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in &documents {\n        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        shard_documents.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    // Distribute shards across nodes (round-robin for RF=1)\n    let node_count = 3usize;\n    for (shard_id, docs) in shard_documents {\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n        match node_idx {\n            0 => node1_docs.extend(docs),\n            1 => node2_docs.extend(docs),\n            2 => node3_docs.extend(docs),\n            _ => unreachable!(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify all documents are distributed\n    let total: usize = node1_docs.len() + node2_docs.len() + node3_docs.len();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000, \"all 1000 documents should be distributed\");\n\n    // Verify each document can be retrieved (simulated by checking presence in node_docs)\n    for doc in &documents {\n        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % 3;\n\n        let found = match node_idx {\n            0 => node1_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            1 => node2_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            2 => node3_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            _ => false,\n        };\n        assert!(found, \"document {} should be retrievable\", id);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 2: Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node < 20%)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_2_distribution_across_all_nodes() {\n    // With 3 nodes, RF=1, each node should get roughly 33% of documents\n    // Minimum 20% means at least 200 docs out of 1000 per node\n\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let node_count = 3usize;\n\n    // Create 1000 test documents\n    let documents: Vec<String> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| format!(\"doc-{i}\"))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Track distribution\n    let mut node_counts: HashMap<usize, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for id in &documents {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n        *node_counts.entry(node_idx).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Verify each node has at least 20% of documents\n    let min_count = (1000.0 * 0.20) as usize;\n    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n        assert!(\n            *count >= min_count,\n            \"node {} has {} docs, expected at least {} (20%)\",\n            node_idx,\n            count,\n            min_count\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify total is 1000\n    let total: usize = node_counts.values().sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n\n    // With good distribution, each node should have roughly 33% ± 10%\n    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n        let percentage = (*count as f64 / 1000.0) * 100.0;\n        assert!(\n            percentage >= 20.0 && percentage <= 50.0,\n            \"node {} has {:.1}% of documents, expected roughly 33%\",\n            node_idx,\n            percentage\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 3: Batch with one missing primary key → 400, no docs written\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_3_batch_missing_primary_key_rejected() {\n    // Create a batch with one document missing the primary key\n    let documents = json!(\n        [\n            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"Valid doc\"},\n            {\"title\": \"Missing ID\"},  // Missing primary key\n            {\"id\": \"doc-3\", \"title\": \"Another valid doc\"}\n        ]\n    );\n\n    // Simulate validation logic - validation fails before any writes\n    let primary_key = \"id\";\n    let has_missing = documents.as_array()\n        .unwrap()\n        .iter()\n        .any(|doc| doc.get(primary_key).is_none());\n\n    assert!(has_missing, \"batch should have a document missing primary key\");\n\n    // Verify error code would be miroir_primary_key_required\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n\n    // No writes should be attempted - validation happens first\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 4: Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_4_reserved_field_rejection() {\n    // Document with _miroir_shard field\n    let documents = json!(\n        [\n            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"_miroir_shard\": 5, \"title\": \"Invalid doc\"}\n        ]\n    );\n\n    // Simulate validation logic - reserved field check happens before writes\n    let has_reserved = documents.as_array()\n        .unwrap()\n        .iter()\n        .any(|doc| doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some());\n\n    assert!(has_reserved, \"document should have reserved field _miroir_shard\");\n\n    // Verify error code would be miroir_reserved_field\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::ReservedField;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n\n    // No writes should be attempted - reserved field validation happens first\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 5: RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down → success with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_5_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    // Simulate quorum calculation with RG=2, RF=1\n    // Per-group quorum = floor(1/2) + 1 = 1 ACK needed per group\n    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;  // = 1\n\n    // Simulate group 0 ACKs, group 1 down\n    let mut group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    group_acks.insert(0, 1);  // Group 0 met quorum\n    // Group 1 has no ACKs (down)\n\n    // Count groups that met quorum\n    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n\n    // Verify: at least 1 group met quorum → write succeeds\n    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 1, \"at least one group should meet quorum\");\n\n    // The key assertion: write succeeds when at least one group meets quorum\n    // X-Miroir-Degraded header would be set to indicate degraded state\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 6: RG=2, RF=1, both groups down → 503 miroir_no_quorum\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_6_no_quorum_both_groups_down() {\n    // Simulate quorum calculation with RG=2, RF=1\n    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;  // = 1\n\n    // No groups ACK (both down)\n    let group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Count groups that met quorum\n    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n\n    // Verify: no groups met quorum → write fails with miroir_no_quorum\n    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 0, \"no groups should meet quorum\");\n\n    // Verify error code\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::NoQuorum;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_no_quorum\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 503);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 7: DELETE by IDs array routes each ID to its shard independently\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_7_delete_by_ids_independent_shard_routing() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Two documents on different shards\n    let doc_a_id = \"user:123\";  // Will route to some shard\n    let doc_b_id = \"product:456\";  // Will route to different shard\n\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let shard_a = shard_for_key(doc_a_id, shard_count);\n    let shard_b = shard_for_key(doc_b_id, shard_count);\n\n    // Verify they're on different shards for this test\n    assert_ne!(shard_a, shard_b, \"test documents should be on different shards\");\n\n    // Mock delete endpoints - we should see independent calls per shard\n    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes/test-index/documents/delete\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n        .expect_at_least(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes/test-index/documents/delete\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n        .expect_at_least(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Simulate grouping IDs by shard\n    let ids = vec![doc_a_id.to_string(), doc_b_id.to_string()];\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for id in &ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    // Verify each shard gets its own delete request\n    assert_eq!(shard_ids.len(), 2, \"should have 2 independent shard delete requests\");\n    assert!(shard_ids.contains_key(&shard_a), \"should have delete request for shard A\");\n    assert!(shard_ids.contains_key(&shard_b), \"should have delete request for shard B\");\n\n    // Verify each shard has the correct document\n    let docs_for_shard_a = shard_ids.get(&shard_a).unwrap();\n    let docs_for_shard_b = shard_ids.get(&shard_b).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_a.len(), 1, \"shard A should have 1 document\");\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_b.len(), 1, \"shard B should have 1 document\");\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_a[0], doc_a_id, \"shard A should have doc A\");\n    assert_eq!(docs_for_shard_b[0], doc_b_id, \"shard B should have doc B\");\n\n    mock1.assert_async().await;\n    mock2.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Additional: DELETE by filter broadcasts to all nodes\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn delete_by_filter_broadcasts_to_all_nodes() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Delete by filter should broadcast to ALL nodes (cannot shard-route)\n    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes/test-index/documents/delete\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes/test-index/documents/delete\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Simulate broadcast to all nodes\n    let nodes = vec![server1.url(), server2.url()];\n    assert_eq!(nodes.len(), 2, \"broadcast should reach all nodes\");\n\n    mock1.assert_async().await;\n    mock2.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Unit tests for quorum calculation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_calculation_rf1() {\n    // RF=1: quorum = floor(1/2) + 1 = 1\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n    assert_eq!(quorum, 1);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_calculation_rf2() {\n    // RF=2: quorum = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2\n    let rf = 2usize;\n    let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n    assert_eq!(quorum, 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_calculation_rf3() {\n    // RF=3: quorum = floor(3/2) + 1 = 2\n    let rf = 3usize;\n    let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n    assert_eq!(quorum, 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_success_at_least_one_group() {\n    // With RG=2, RF=1, write succeeds if at least 1 group meets quorum\n    let _replica_groups = 2u32;\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n    let mut group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    group_acks.insert(0, 1);  // Group 0 met quorum\n    group_acks.insert(1, 0);  // Group 1 didn't\n\n    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"write should succeed with at least 1 group at quorum\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_failure_no_groups() {\n    // With RG=2, RF=1, write fails if NO groups meet quorum\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n    let group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();  // No ACKs\n\n    let quorum_groups = group_acks.values().filter(|&&acks| acks >= quorum_per_group).count();\n    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 0, \"write should fail with no groups at quorum\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Unit tests for shard distribution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_shard_for_key_deterministic() {\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let key = \"test-key\";\n\n    let shard1 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n    let shard2 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n\n    assert_eq!(shard1, shard2, \"shard_for_key should be deterministic\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_shard_for_key_within_range() {\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        let key = format!(\"key-{}\", i);\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n        assert!(shard < shard_count, \"shard ID should be within range\");\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_shard_distribution_evenness() {\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let doc_count = 1000;\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for i in 0..doc_count {\n        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n        *shard_counts.entry(shard).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    // With 1000 docs and 8 shards, ideal is 125 docs per shard\n    // Allow ±50% variance (62-187 docs per shard)\n    let min_expected = (125.0 * 0.5) as usize;\n    let max_expected = (125.0 * 1.5) as usize;\n\n    for (shard, count) in &shard_counts {\n        assert!(\n            *count >= min_expected && *count <= max_expected,\n            \"shard {} has {} docs, expected between {} and {}\",\n            shard,\n            count,\n            min_expected,\n            max_expected\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify total\n    let total: usize = shard_counts.values().sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, doc_count);\n}\n","numLines":489,"startLine":1,"totalLines":489}}}

P2.2 Write path acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the complete write path implementation:\n//! - POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - add documents\n//! - PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - replace documents\n//! - DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - delete by ID\n//! - DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - delete by IDs array or filter\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! 1. 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n//! 2. Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n//! 3. Batch with one missing primary key → 400 miroir_primary_key_required, no docs written anywhere\n//! 4. Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n//! 5. RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n//! 6. RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 miroir_no_quorum\n//! 7. DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse miroir_core::config::NodeConfig;\nuse miroir_core::router::shard_for_key;\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\nfn make_config(shards: u32, rf: u32, replica_groups: u32, node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> MiroirConfig {\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeConfig> = node_addresses\n        .into_iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, addr)| NodeConfig {\n            id: format!(\"node-{i}\"),\n            address: addr,\n            replica_group: (i % replica_groups as usize) as u32,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n        shards,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        replica_groups,\n        nodes,\n        ..Default::default()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 1: 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_1_1000_docs_indexed_retrievable() {\n    // Verify the orchestrator correctly distributes 1000 documents across shards\n    // and that each document can be retrieved from its assigned shard\n\n    // Track which documents each node receives\n    let mut node1_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut node2_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut node3_docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n\n    // Create 1000 test documents\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| json!({\"id\": format!(\"doc-{i}\"), \"title\": format!(\"Document {i}\")}))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate the orchestrator's shard grouping logic\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<serde_json::Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in &documents {\n        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        shard_documents.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    // Distribute shards across nodes (round-robin for RF=1)\n    let node_count = 3usize;\n    for (shard_id, docs) in shard_documents {\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n        match node_idx {\n            0 => node1_docs.extend(docs),\n            1 => node2_docs.extend(docs),\n            2 => node3_docs.extend(docs),\n            _ => unreachable!(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify all documents are distributed\n    let total: usize = node1_docs.len() + node2_docs.len() + node3_docs.len();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000, \"all 1000 documents should be distributed\");\n\n    // Verify each document can be retrieved (simulated by checking presence in node_docs)\n    for doc in &documents {\n        let id = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap();\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % 3;\n\n        let found = match node_idx {\n            0 => node1_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            1 => node2_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            2 => node3_docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(id)),\n            _ => false,\n        };\n        assert!(found, \"document {} should be retrievable\", id);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 2: Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node < 20%)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn acceptance_2_distribution_across_all_nodes() {\n    // With 3 nodes, RF=1, each node should get roughly 33% of documents\n    // Minimum 20% means at least 200 docs out of 1000 per node\n\n    let shard_count = 8u32;\n    let node_count = 3usize;\n\n    // Create 1000 test documents\n    let documents: Vec<String> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| format!(\"doc-{i}\"))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Track distribution\n    let mut node_counts: HashMap<usize, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for id in &documents {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        let node_idx = shard_id as usize % node_count;\n        *node_counts.entry(node_idx).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Verify each node has at least 20% of documents\n    let min_count = (1000.0 * 0.20) as usize;\n    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n        assert!(\n            *count >= min_count,\n            \"node {} has {} docs, expected at least {} (20%)\",\n            node_idx,\n            count,\n            min_count\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify total is 1000\n    let total: usize = node_counts.values().sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n\n    // With good distribution, each node should have roughly 33% ± 10%\n    for (node_idx, count) in &node_counts {\n        let percentage = (*count as f64 / 1000.0) * 100.0;\n        assert!(\n            percentage >= 20.0 && percentage <= 50.0,\n            \"node {} has {:.1}% of documents, expected roughly 33%\",\n            node_idx,\n            percentage\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 3: Batch with one missing primary key → 400, no docs written\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_3_batch_missing_primary_key_rejected() {\n    // Create a batch with one document missing the primary key\n    let documents = json!(\n        [\n            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"title\": \"Valid doc\"},\n            {\"title\": \"Missing ID\"},  // Missing primary key\n            {\"id\": \"doc-3\", \"title\": \"Another valid doc\"}\n        ]\n    );\n\n    // Simulate validation logic - validation fails before any writes\n    let primary_key = \"id\";\n    let has_missing = documents.as_array()\n        .unwrap()\n        .iter()\n        .any(|doc| doc.get(primary_key).is_none());\n\n    assert!(has_missing, \"batch should have a document missing primary key\");\n\n    // Verify error code would be miroir_primary_key_required\n    use miroir_core::api_error::MiroirCode;\n    let code = MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n\n    // No writes should be attempted - validation happens first\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance 4: Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn acceptance_4_reserved_field_rejection() {\n    // Document with _miroir_shard field\n    let documents = json!(\n        [\n            {\"id\": \"doc-1\", \"_miroir_shard\": 5, \"title\": \"Invalid doc\"}\n        ]\n    );\n\n    // Simulate validation logic - reserved field check happens before
